what_it_does: This file is a ledger terrain-entry that documents and interprets 'creations' (VECO), a declarative TypeScript 'universal circuit engine' scaffold found at /home/tim/creations, describing its structure, key ideas, relationship to the Company, read coverage, and current status.
observations: 13 of 13 files were read; coverage is marked complete.
The project is a scaffold/specification only: types, circuit data, instance data, and named-string handler references, with no handler bodies, runtime, package.json, or build tooling.
It defines three circuit shapes: simple (linear 6-stage), conditional (complexity branch), and MCP (tool discovery/binding/execution stages).
The invariant stage flow is Principal → Domain → Intent → Proposal → Approval → Execution.
It includes a dual-provider router concept (Anthropic primary, Ollama Cloud fallback) driven by task complexity and token requirements, with per-stage model selection.
MCP is modeled as first-class circuit stages with explicit binding/approval/execution.
A 'genesis-001' instance is described as the engine running on its own initialization.
The content is wrapped in a game/narrative fiction ('Chapter 3: The Living World', 'Quest #7: The Genesis Event', NPC-authored).
No secrets are present; API keys are referenced only via environment variable names.
Created and last active on 2026-03-01; 112K; no git remote.
Status is 'unconfirmed' and noted as a scaffold fact for Tim to confirm.
Two divergent config drafts exist (root snake_case/flat vs nested camelCase/per-stage providerConfig).
standouts: The literal six-stage relational circuit is treated as a reusable primitive across all variants.
Dual-provider, complexity/token-driven routing with per-stage model selection.
MCP tool discovery/binding/execution modeled as explicit circuit stages.
Genesis event as self-execution of the engine on its own initialization.
Scaffold-only: declarative specs with no runtime or handlers.
conventions: Follows the ledger YAML frontmatter schema (type, register, coverage, relation, kind, era, domain_ref, themes, concepts, tags).
Uses [[wiki-style]] cross-references in relates-to and prospected-for.
Quotes exact file paths and lines as evidence.
Marks speculative/lifecycle facts with status: unconfirmed and explicit Notes.
Uses neutral, descriptive register without implementation claims in the frontmatter.
concerns: Status is 'unconfirmed' and the body notes it is a scaffold fact awaiting Tim confirmation.
No runtime or handler implementations exist, so some descriptions may read as more concrete than the code actually is.
Two config drafts with different conventions (snake_case vs camelCase, flat vs nested) could create ambiguity about which is authoritative.
The narrative/game-fiction wrapper may obscure the technical intent for readers unfamiliar with the framing.
No package.json or build tooling means integration mechanics are undefined.
notes: A ledger interpretation of an early declarative circuit-engine scaffold whose main value is the reusable six-stage flow and role-resolved model-routing ideas, not a working runtime.
questions: Has Tim confirmed the lifecycle status of this scaffold?
Will the named-string handlers be implemented, and where?
Which of the two dual-provider config drafts is authoritative?
How would the router resolve providers without a runtime or package.json?
Are the MCP tool bindings tied to real servers or purely speculative?
How, if at all, is this scaffold intended to connect to actual Company code?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to document and interpret a terrain-entry for the creations scaffold; the file does exactly that, including noting where it is speculative rather than implemented.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to capture an agent-built instantiation of Tim's relational-systems doctrine as a typed, reusable circuit primitive, while also prototyping role-resolved model routing and MCP-aware execution as first-class circuit concerns.
novelty: A universal six-stage circuit reused across simple, conditional, and MCP variants; per-stage model selection inside a single circuit; MCP tool discovery/binding/execution treated as circuit stages; a genesis instance that runs the engine on itself.
contribution: It contributes a concrete expression of a typed staged-circuit primitive, a dual-provider/per-stage model routing pattern, an MCP-as-circuit-stage pattern, and a self-execution genesis concept that a fused system could evaluate and adopt if aligned.
summary_for_embedding: A complete-read ledger entry for 'creations' (VECO), a declarative TypeScript universal-circuit-engine scaffold with simple, conditional, and MCP circuit shapes, dual-provider routing, per-stage model selection, and a genesis self-execution instance, but no runtime or handlers.
intention_for_embedding: This file was written to record and interpret an early scaffold as a mineable conceptual resource for the Company's composition, model-routing, and MCP integration work.
